title: Charmed Life - Chapter 9 (Fragment 4)
text:
Cat gasped at the way she spoke. Chrestomanci seemed perplexed. “How are you not having it?” he said.

“I won’t put up with it!” Gwendolen shouted at him. “In future, my letters are going to come to me closed!”

“You mean you want me to steam them open and stick them down afterward?” Chrestomanci asked doubtfully. “It’s more trouble, but I’ll do that if it makes you happier.”

Gwendolen stared at him. “You mean you did it? You read a letter addressed to me ?”

Chrestomanci nodded blandly. “Naturally. If someone like Henry Nostrum writes letters to you, I have to make sure he’s not writing anything unsuitable. He’s a very seedy person.”

“He was my teacher!” Gwendolen said furiously. “You’ve no right to!”

“It’s a pity,” said Chrestomanci, “that you were taught by a hedge wizard. You’ll have to unlearn such a lot. And it’s a pity too that I’ve no right to open your letters. I hope you don’t get many, or my conscience will give me no peace.”

“You intend to go on?” Gwendolen said. “Then watch out. I warn you!”

“That is very considerate of you,” said Chrestomanci. “I like to be warned.” He came up the rest of the marble stairs and went past Gwendolen and Cat. The pink and orange dressing gown swirled, revealing a bright scarlet lining. Cat blinked.

Gwendolen stared vengefully as the dazzling dressing gown flowed away along the gallery. “Oh no, don’t notice me, will you!” she said. “Make jokes. You wait! Cat, I’m so furious!”

“You were awfully rude,” said Cat.

“He deserved it,” said Gwendolen, and began to hurry back towards the playroom. “Opening poor Mr. Nostrum’s letter! It isn’t that I mind him reading it. We arranged a code, so horrid Chrestomanci will never know what it’s really saying, but there is the signature. But it’s the insult. The indignity. I’m at their mercy in this Castle. I’m all on my own in distress and I can’t even stop them reading my letters. But I’ll show them. You wait!”

Cat knew better than to say anything. Gwendolen slammed into the playroom, flounced down at the table, and began at last to read her letter.

“I told you so,” said Euphemia, while Mary was working the lift.

Gwendolen shot her a look. “You wait too,” she said, and went on reading. After a bit, she looked in the envelope again. “There’s one for you too,” she said to Cat, and tossed him a sheet of paper. “Mind you reply to it.”

Cat took it, wondering nervously why Mr. Nostrum should write to him. But it was from Mrs. Sharp. She wrote:

Me dear Cat,

Ow are you doin then me love? I fine meself lonesum an missin you both particular you the place seems so quiete. Thourght I was lookin forward to a bit peace but missin yer voice an wishin you was comin in bringin appels. One thing happen an that was a gennelman come an give five poun for the ole cat that was yer fidel so I feel flush and had idear of packin you up a parsel of jinjerbredmen and mebbe bringin them to you one of these days but Mr. Nostrum sez not to. Spect your in the lap of luckshury anyhows. Love to Gwendolen. Wish you was back here Cat and the money means nothin.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that is calm, unfriendly, or shows no strong feelings.
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci nodded blandly.

vocabulary.definition: A system of words, letters, or signs used to represent a message secretly.
vocabulary.example: We arranged a code.

vocabulary.definition: An inner feeling or voice viewed as acting as a guide to rightness or wrongness of one's behaviour.
vocabulary.example: My conscience will give me no peace.

vocabulary.definition: Showing concern for the needs or feelings of others.
vocabulary.example: That is very considerate of you.

vocabulary.definition: Extremely impressive, beautiful, or skillful.
vocabulary.example: The dazzling dressing gown flowed away.

vocabulary.definition: Extreme anxiety, sorrow, or pain.
vocabulary.example: I’m all on my own in distress.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows uncertainty or hesitation.
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci asked doubtfully.

vocabulary.definition: A loose robe worn over night clothes for warmth or modesty.
vocabulary.example: The pink and orange dressing gown swirled.

vocabulary.definition: To move in an exaggeratedly impatient or angry manner.
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen flounced down at the table.

vocabulary.definition: Having plenty of money or resources.
vocabulary.example: I feel flush.

vocabulary.definition: In a very angry or furious way.
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen said furiously.

vocabulary.definition: A derogatory term for a wizard practicing ineffective or unorthodox magic.
vocabulary.example: You were taught by a hedge wizard.

vocabulary.definition: Treatment or circumstances that cause one to feel shame or to lose one's dignity.
vocabulary.example: But it’s the insult. The indignity.

vocabulary.definition: A remark or action that is rude or offensive.
vocabulary.example: But it’s the insult.

vocabulary.definition: Lonely, solitary (dialect spelling of 'lonesome').
vocabulary.example: I fine meself lonesum an missin you both.

vocabulary.definition: In a worried or uneasy manner.
vocabulary.example: Wondering nervously why he should get a letter.

vocabulary.definition: A package or bundle of things wrapped up together.
vocabulary.example: Packin you up a parcel of gingerbread men.

vocabulary.definition: Completely baffled; very puzzled.
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci seemed perplexed.

vocabulary.definition: A feeling of sadness or sympathy for someone else's misfortune.
vocabulary.example: It’s a pity that you were taught by a hedge wizard.

vocabulary.definition: A room set aside for children to play in.
vocabulary.example: She hurried back towards the playroom.

vocabulary.definition: To say, write, or do something as an answer to something else.
vocabulary.example: Mind you reply to it.

vocabulary.definition: A bright red colour.
vocabulary.example: Revealing a bright scarlet lining.

vocabulary.definition: Looking dirty or in bad condition, or morally disreputable.
vocabulary.example: He’s a very seedy person.

vocabulary.definition: To say something very loudly, usually because you are angry or excited.
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen shouted at him.

vocabulary.definition: A person's name written in a distinctive way as a form of identification.
vocabulary.example: But there is the signature.

vocabulary.definition: To open envelopes by applying steam to seal them gently.
vocabulary.example: You want me to steam them open.

vocabulary.definition: To throw something lightly or casually.
vocabulary.example: She tossed him a sheet of paper.

vocabulary.definition: Not appropriate or fitting for a particular purpose or occasion.
vocabulary.example: Make sure he’s not writing anything unsuitable.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows a desire for revenge.
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen stared vengefully.
